title: polypodium lineare subsp. ussuriense
intro: Polypodium lineare subsp. ussuriense, also known as Ussuri Polypody, is a type of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its linear leaves and creeping rhizomes. Traditionally, Ussuri Polypody has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and potentially lowering blood pressure. In herbal preparations, Ussuri Polypody is often combined with other plants and used as a decoction or tea, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
